title: entada phaneroneura
intro: Entada phaneroneura, also known as the 'elephant ear tree', is a climbing plant native to Australia and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Entada phaneroneura include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and support the immune system. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used in traditional medicine, and can be prepared as teas, tinctures, or infusions for therapeutic use.
